flags.concern: Timeline selection clearing: the plan does not cover plain clicks on empty timeline space. `useMarqueeSelect` clears selection by calling `clearSelection()` directly when a non-additive pointer down/up does not become a drag, so under the proposed changes gallery selection would survive an empty-background click in the timeline.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useMarqueeSelect.ts:156-170` calls `clearSelection()` for the no-move, no-modifier path, while the plan only cross-clears gallery from `selectClip`/`selectClips` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts:66-92` and from Escape in `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx`. That leaves requirement (4) unmet for plain timeline background clicks.

flags.concern: Timeline selection sync: the plan misses timeline-side selection changes that come through `selectedClipId` synchronization rather than `selectClip`. Several editor actions update `selectedClipId` via `applyResolvedConfigEdit`, and `useTimelineState` then replays that into multi-select with `selectClipState(...)`, bypassing the proposed `clearGallerySelection()` wrapper.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts:134-140` syncs `dataHook.selectedClipId` with `selectClipState(...)` directly. Current callers such as `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts:306-329` (split selects the new clip) and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:180-183` (move-to-track reselects the clip) update `selectedClipId` through `applyResolvedConfigEdit`. Those flows would still leave gallery items selected, violating the unified-selection invariant outside the narrow click paths.
